evidence.description: Al-Maliki spent years in exile in Iran during Saddam's rule, cultivating close ties with the Islamic Republic leadership. During his tenure as Prime Minister, Iranian support — including from Supreme Leader Khamenei — was decisive in enabling him to retain power after the 2010 elections.

editorial_notes: Al-Maliki's Iran ties run deep — he lived in Iranian exile and has consistently aligned with Tehran's interests. Khamenei reportedly backed him during the 2010 government formation crisis.
